methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $465M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Photonic Inc, a Series E quantum computing company founded by Stephanie Simmons and Paul Terry, is valued within a sector containing 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 5 private peers including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion. The company has raised $375M CAD to date.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using total capital raised of $465M as a valuation proxy, a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement yields an implied value of $651M. This approach assumes forward funding progress and market conditions consistent with historical stage-to-stage appreciation patterns in the quantum computing sector.

Peer-median benchmarking could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds; the broader peer set lacks sufficient homogeneity to support direct valuation cross-reference without material distortion risk. The analysis therefore supports a range of $553M (conservative discount) to $749M (upside scenario), with a median estimate of $651M. Key uncertainties include the availability of tighter-stage peer multiples, near-term funding environment conditions, and relative technology maturity versus the identified peer group.
